sources.claim: OpenTelemetry provides standards and tools to collect metrics, logs, and traces.
OpenTelemetry semantic conventions define common names for operations and data across telemetry signals.
OpenTelemetry event semantics require event names that uniquely identify event structure and recommend attributes for context.
W3C Trace Context defines traceparent/tracestate for propagating trace identity across systems.
SLSA provenance is verifiable information describing where, when, and how an artifact was produced.

design_decisions: Model every boot/stage/validator/export/receipt/handoff as an event with a stable event_name and typed attributes.
Use trace_id/span_id/parent_span_id to allow causal grouping without requiring network headers.
Bind outputs to input_materials/provenance rather than summary-only state.
Keep raw JSONL as canonical evidence; generated indexes and graphs are derivative artifacts.
